DISCUSSION
On the assumption that our observations have supported and enlarged Golding's initial observation, it seems logical to approach the pathomechanics of the development of this finding.

It is doubtful whether this upward migration of the humeral head can occur as an early change in rotator cuff tears, except perhaps when there is complete avulsion of the cuff with retraction of the s

SUMMARY
1. A radiological study of the acromio-humeral interval in sixty normal shoulders and in fifty-nine shoulders with known tears of the rotator cuff is reported.
evidence: ["reference-like pattern: 1. A radiological study of the acromio-humeral interval in s"]

content_preview: 2. The normal acromio-humeral interval was found to be seven to fourteen millimetres, a range that is consistent with previous observations.
evidence: ["reference-like pattern: 2. The normal acromio-humeral interval was found to be seven"]

content_preview: 3. Narrowing of this interval is a frequent concomitant of a tear of the rotator cuff.
evidence: ["reference-like pattern: 3. Narrowing of this interval is a frequent concomitant of a"]

content_preview: 4. An interval of five millimetres or less should be considered compatible with a tear of the rotator cuff until proven otherwise.
evidence: ["reference-like pattern: 4. An interval of five millimetres or less should be conside"]
